original_hypothesis.title: Arbitrary Token Recovery Dilutes asdUSDC Backing
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The recover function lacks a whitelist check, allowing a compromised owner to mint asdUSDC backed by worthless tokens and drain legitimate reserves.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Assessing the risk of privilege escalation via the unrestricted recover function.
preconditions: Owner key is compromised or admin acts maliciously

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Minting core protocol tokens based on the balance of unverified external contracts
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time the owner key is compromised

operational_questions: Is the owner a multisig or an EOA?
Are there any off-chain monitoring systems for the recover function?
validation_requirements: Call recover with a non-whitelisted mock token
Verify that asdUSDC is minted successfully

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker gains access to the owner key
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker sends a worthless ERC20 token to the ASDUSDC contract
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Attacker calls recover with the worthless token's address
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Contract mints asdUSDC backed by the worthless token, which the attacker can swap for real USDC

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Loss of all user funds in asdUSDC
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires hard fork or external capital injection

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Calls to recover with unknown token addresses
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Massive inflation of asdUSDC total supply
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Immediate withdrawal of real USDC following a recover call

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
